author: Sangharakshita
title: Contents of New Zealanders in the UK 1985 - Questions and Answers
content:
SANGHARAKSHITA IN SEMINAR

QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS WITH NEW ZEALANDERS - 1985
CONTENTS
1Clarifying the positive nidanas from 'The Three Jewels'
2Triumphalism
A triumph of the spirit despite material disaster
5New Zealand national characteristics
11These characteristics in relation to the FWBO
Pioneering spirit
12The strengths and weaknesses of naivety
Being as harmless as the dove and as wise as the serpent
The difficulties of individualism
13Having more open air activities where climate permits
14Culture. Too much culture
15Dharma study as an integral part of the FWBO
The need for a thorough understanding of the Dharma
16Specializing in one Buddhist text or subject
17Recommendations for overseas visitors to FWBO in the UK
Three things to give absolute priority to:- Meditation, Dharma study and human
communication.
19'Science' and 'Mysticism'
Can Stream Entrants be found outside historical Buddhism?
20Sufism and Taoism.
